entries.name: Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes
entries.desc: Argentina's national fine arts museum, holding the country's most important public art collection, spanning Argentine artists and European masters from the Renaissance to the modern era.

photo.alt: Exterior facade of the Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes in Buenos Aires
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museo-nacional-de-bellas-artes-buenos-aires.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC-BY-2.0 · "Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes (Buenos Aires) 10209.jpg" by Brian Barbutti · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museo_Nacional_de_Bellas_Artes_(Buenos_Aires)_10209.jpg -->

entries.name: National Gallery of Australia
entries.desc: Australia's national art museum, holding the country's largest art collection. It is especially strong in Australian and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander art, alongside international works.

photo.alt: National Gallery of Australia building in Canberra viewed from the southwest
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-gallery-of-australia-canberra.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC-BY-SA 4.0 · "National Gallery from SW, Canberra Australia.jpg" by Thennicke · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Gallery_from_SW,_Canberra_Australia.jpg -->

entries.name: Kunsthistorisches Museum
entries.desc: Built to house the imperial Habsburg collections, it holds major Old Master paintings, including the world's largest group of works by Pieter Bruegel the Elder.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Kunsthistorisches Museum on Maria-Theresien-Platz in Vienna
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kunsthistorisches-museum-vienna.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC-BY-2.0 · "Wien - Kunsthistorisches Museum.jpg" by Fred Romero · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wien_-_Kunsthistorisches_Museum.jpg -->

entries.name: Belvedere
entries.tag: Vienna · Austrian Art · Klimt's The Kiss
entries.desc: Set in a Baroque palace complex, it holds the largest collection of Gustav Klimt paintings, centered on The Kiss, alongside Austrian art from the medieval era onward.

photo.alt: Upper Belvedere palace exterior in Vienna with reflecting pool
photo.credit: <!-- photo: belvedere-palace-vienna.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC-BY-SA 2.0 DE · "Upper Belvedere palace Vienna.jpg" by Wolfgang Zablatzky · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Upper_Belvedere_palace_Vienna.jpg -->

entries.name: Albertina
entries.tag: Vienna · Graphic Arts · Monet to Picasso
entries.desc: Housed in a former Habsburg residence, it holds one of the world's largest and most important collections of prints and drawings, including works by Dürer alongside modern painting.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Albertina museum in Vienna with the Soravia Wing canopy
photo.credit: <!-- photo: albertina-vienna.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC-BY-SA 4.0 · "Wien - Albertina (b).JPG" by C.Stadler/Bwag · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wien_-_Albertina_(b).JPG -->

entries.name: MASP — São Paulo Museum of Art
entries.desc: A landmark of Brazilian modernism by Lina Bo Bardi, its main volume raised on red pillars over an open plaza. It holds the most significant collection of European art in the Southern Hemisphere.

photo.alt: MASP building on Avenida Paulista raised on its signature red pillars
photo.credit: <!-- photo: masp-sao-paulo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "MASP Brazil.jpg" by Wilfredor · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:MASP_Brazil.jpg -->

entries.name: National Gallery of Canada
entries.tag: Ottawa · Canadian Art · European Old Masters
entries.desc: Canada's national art museum, housed in a glass-and-granite building by Moshe Safdie. It holds the country's largest collection of Canadian art, including works by the Group of Seven.

photo.alt: National Gallery of Canada glass-and-granite building viewed from Major's Hill Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-gallery-of-canada-ottawa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC-BY-SA 3.0 · "National Gallery of Canada in Ottawa.jpg" by Jcart1534 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Gallery_of_Canada_in_Ottawa.jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of China
entries.desc: One of the world's largest museums by floor area, on the east side of Tiananmen Square. Its permanent galleries trace Chinese history and civilization from prehistory to the present.

photo.alt: West facade of the National Museum of China on Tiananmen Square, Beijing
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-of-china.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "National Museum of China west facade, straight view.jpg" by Daniel Case · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Museum_of_China_west_facade,_straight_view.jpg -->

entries.name: Louvre Museum
entries.desc: The world's most visited museum, housed in a former royal palace on the Seine. Its collection spans antiquity to the mid-19th century, anchored by the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo.

photo.alt: Louvre Pyramid entrance in the Cour Napoléon, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: louvre-pyramid.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "La Pyramide du Louvre.JPG" by Briqus Orven Rémi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:La_Pyramide_du_Louvre.JPG -->

entries.name: Musée d'Orsay
entries.desc: Set in a former Beaux-Arts railway station, it holds the world's largest collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces, including works by Monet, Renoir, Degas, and Van Gogh.

photo.alt: Musée d'Orsay's Beaux-Arts facade, the former Gare d'Orsay railway station, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: musee-dorsay-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Exterior of the Musée d'Orsay 20171222.jpg" by Suicasmo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Exterior_of_the_Mus%C3%A9e_d%27Orsay_20171222.jpg -->

entries.name: Centre Pompidou
entries.desc: Known for its radical inside-out high-tech architecture, it houses Europe's largest collection of modern and contemporary art, spanning the 20th century to today.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Centre Pompidou in Paris at night, showing its exposed structural pipes and escalator tubes
photo.credit: <!-- photo: centre-pompidou.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Pompidou Centre.jpg" by Infinitescreen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pompidou_Centre.jpg -->

entries.name: Musée de l'Orangerie
entries.desc: A former royal greenhouse in the Tuileries, best known for two oval rooms displaying Monet's monumental Water Lilies murals, alongside an Impressionist and Post-Impressionist collection.

photo.alt: Musée de l'Orangerie, the former royal greenhouse in the Tuileries, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: musee-orangerie-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Musée de l'Orangerie, Paris 28 May 2017.jpg" by Xiquinho Silva · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mus%C3%A9e_de_l%27Orangerie,_Paris_28_May_2017.jpg -->

entries.name: Musée Rodin
entries.desc: Dedicated to the sculptor Auguste Rodin, set in the 18th-century Hôtel Biron and its gardens. Highlights include The Thinker, The Kiss, and The Gates of Hell.

photo.alt: The Thinker sculpture in the garden of Musée Rodin, with the Hôtel Biron behind it, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: musee-rodin-thinker-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Musée Rodin 1.jpg" by CrisNYCa · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mus%C3%A9e_Rodin_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Pergamon Museum
entries.tag: Berlin · Ancient Near Eastern · Classical Antiquities
entries.desc: Part of Berlin's Museum Island, it is renowned for monumental reconstructed structures, including the Pergamon Altar and the Ishtar Gate of ancient Babylon.

photo.alt: Front facade and entrance plaza of the Pergamon Museum on Museum Island, Berlin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: pergamon-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Pergamonmuseum Front.jpg" by Raimond Spekking · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pergamonmuseum_Front.jpg -->

entries.name: Alte Nationalgalerie
entries.desc: A temple-like building on Museum Island housing 19th-century art, from German Romanticism and the works of Caspar David Friedrich to French and German Impressionism.

photo.alt: Alte Nationalgalerie's temple-like exterior on Museum Island, Berlin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: alte-nationalgalerie-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Alte Nationalgalerie 1.JPG" by Klaus Bärwinkel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Alte_Nationalgalerie_1.JPG -->

entries.name: Israel Museum
entries.desc: Israel's largest cultural institution, spanning archaeology and fine art. Its Shrine of the Book displays the Dead Sea Scrolls, and the grounds include a model of Second Temple-era Jerusalem.

photo.alt: Exterior building of the Israel Museum campus in Jerusalem with the Anish Kapoor sculpture in the plaza
photo.credit: <!-- photo: israel-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Israel museum exterior.jpg" by Igor Zeiger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Israel_museum_exterior.jpg -->

entries.name: Uffizi Gallery
entries.desc: Housed in a 16th-century Medici administrative building, it holds one of the finest collections of Italian Renaissance painting, including Botticelli's Birth of Venus and Primavera.

photo.alt: The Uffizi Gallery seen from across the Arno river, Florence
photo.credit: <!-- photo: uffizi-gallery-florence.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Uffizi Gallery from south 2013 February.jpg" by Morio · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Uffizi_Gallery_from_south_2013_February.jpg -->

entries.name: Vatican Museums
entries.desc: A vast complex displaying art amassed by the popes over centuries. The route culminates in the Raphael Rooms and Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel ceiling.

photo.alt: Entrance to the Vatican Museums beneath the Vatican walls
photo.credit: <!-- photo: vatican-museums-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Musei Vaticani - Esterno.JPG" by Pufui Pc Pifpef I · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Musei_Vaticani_-_Esterno.JPG -->

entries.name: Galleria dell'Accademia
entries.desc: Best known as the home of Michelangelo's original David, moved here in 1873. It also holds his unfinished Prisoners and a collection of Florentine paintings.

photo.alt: Entrance to the Galleria dell'Accademia, Florence
photo.credit: <!-- photo: galleria-dellaccademia-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Entrance of Galleria dell'Accademia.JPG" by Ctny · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_of_Galleria_dell%27Accademia.JPG -->

entries.name: Galleria Borghese
entries.desc: A former cardinal's villa set in landscaped gardens, renowned for its Bernini sculptures and Caravaggio paintings. Timed entry limits visitor numbers to preserve the intimate galleries.

photo.alt: Facade of the Villa Borghese Pinciana, home of the Galleria Borghese, Rome
photo.credit: <!-- photo: galleria-borghese-villa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Galerie Borghèse - Rome (IT62) - 2021-08-30 - 3.jpg" by Chabe01 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Galerie_Borgh%C3%A8se_-_Rome_(IT62)_-_2021-08-30_-_3.jpg -->

entries.name: Pinacoteca di Brera
entries.desc: Milan's main public gallery, occupying a grand palazzo shared with an art academy. It is celebrated for northern Italian painting, including works by Raphael, Mantegna, and Piero della Francesca.

photo.alt: Arcaded courtyard of the Palazzo Brera, Milan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: palazzo-brera-courtyard.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cour Intérieure Palais Brera - Milan (IT25) - 2022-09-03 - 1.jpg" by Chabe01 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cour_Int%C3%A9rieure_Palais_Brera_-_Milan_(IT25)_-_2022-09-03_-_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Tokyo National Museum
entries.desc: Japan's oldest and largest museum, set in Ueno Park. It holds the country's most comprehensive collection of Japanese art and antiquities, including numerous designated National Treasures.

photo.alt: Main building (Honkan) of the Tokyo National Museum in Ueno Park, Tokyo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tokyo-national-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Main building of the Tokyo National Museum 20170208.jpg" by Suicasmo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Main_building_of_the_Tokyo_National_Museum_20170208.jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of Western Art
entries.tag: Tokyo · Western Art · UNESCO World Heritage
entries.desc: Japan's principal museum of Western art, built around the Matsukata collection. Its Le Corbusier-designed main building is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

photo.alt: Le Corbusier-designed main building of the National Museum of Western Art, Tokyo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-western-art-tokyo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "National Museum Of Western Art (218675051).jpeg" by Francisco Anzola · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Museum_Of_Western_Art_(218675051).jpeg -->

entries.name: Rijksmuseum
entries.desc: The Netherlands' national museum, devoted to Dutch art and history. Its Gallery of Honour centers on Rembrandt's The Night Watch and paintings by Vermeer and Frans Hals.

photo.alt: The Rijksmuseum's twin towers and central passage, Amsterdam
photo.credit: <!-- photo: rijksmuseum-amsterdam.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Netherlands-4131 - Rijksmuseum (11714501335).jpg" by Dennis G. Jarvis · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Netherlands-4131_-_Rijksmuseum_(11714501335).jpg -->

entries.name: Van Gogh Museum
entries.desc: Holds the world's largest collection of works by Vincent van Gogh, including paintings, drawings, and letters, tracing his career alongside works by his contemporaries.

photo.alt: Van Gogh Museum exterior, Amsterdam
photo.credit: <!-- photo: van-gogh-museum-amsterdam.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Van Gogh Museum Amsterdam" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Van_Gogh_Museum_Amsterdam.jpg -->

entries.name: Museum of Islamic Art
entries.desc: Designed by I. M. Pei on its own island in Doha Bay, it holds one of the most comprehensive collections of Islamic art, spanning manuscripts, ceramics, metalwork, and textiles across continents.

photo.alt: Museum of Islamic Art on its island in Doha Bay
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-of-islamic-art-doha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "IslamicArtMuseumDohaSkyline" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:IslamicArtMuseumDohaSkyline.jpg -->

entries.name: Hermitage Museum
entries.tag: St. Petersburg · Encyclopedic · European Old Masters
entries.desc: One of the world's largest museums, spread across the former Winter Palace and adjoining buildings. Its holdings span antiquity to modern art, including works by Rembrandt and da Vinci.

photo.alt: Winter Palace, home of the Hermitage Museum, St. Petersburg
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hermitage-museum-winter-palace-st-petersburg.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "00 2038 Winter Palace - Hermitage Museum (St. Petersburg)" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:00_2038_Winter_Palace_-_Hermitage_Museum_(St._Petersburg).jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of Korea
entries.desc: The flagship museum of Korean history and art, and one of the largest in Asia. Its collection ranges from prehistoric artifacts to Buddhist sculpture, celadon ceramics, and calligraphy.

photo.alt: National Museum of Korea exterior, Seoul
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-of-korea-seoul.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Seoul-National.Museum.of.Korea-01.jpg" by Ian Muttoo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Seoul-National.Museum.of.Korea-01.jpg -->

entries.name: Prado Museum
entries.desc: Spain's national art museum, holding the finest collection of Spanish painting anywhere. It is anchored by masterworks from Velázquez, Goya, and El Greco.

photo.alt: Prado Museum building, Madrid
photo.credit: <!-- photo: prado-museum-madrid.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Prado Museum, Madrid" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Prado_Museum,_Madrid.jpg -->

entries.name: Reina Sofía Museum
entries.desc: Spain's national museum of 20th-century art, set in a former hospital. Its centerpiece is Picasso's Guernica, alongside significant holdings of Dalí and Miró.

photo.alt: Reina Sofía Museum building, Madrid
photo.credit: <!-- photo: reina-sofia-museum-madrid.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Museo Reina Sofia, Madrid (6394601959)" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museo_Reina_Sofia,_Madrid_(6394601959).jpg -->

entries.name: Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum
entries.desc: Built around a private collection assembled by the Thyssen-Bornemisza family, it traces Western painting from the medieval period through the 20th century, filling gaps left by the nearby Prado.

photo.alt: Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum building, Madrid
photo.credit: <!-- photo: thyssen-bornemisza-museum-madrid.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Museo Thyssen-Bornemisza (Madrid) 07" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museo_Thyssen-Bornemisza_(Madrid)_07.jpg -->

entries.name: Picasso Museum
entries.desc: Spread across five medieval mansions, it holds one of the most extensive collections of Picasso's work, with a particular focus on his formative years and Barcelona period.

photo.alt: Museu Picasso medieval mansions, Barcelona
photo.credit: <!-- photo: picasso-museum-barcelona.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Museu Picasso Barcelona" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museu_Picasso_Barcelona.JPG -->

entries.name: National Palace Museum
entries.desc: Holds one of the largest collections of Chinese imperial artifacts, drawn from the former Beijing palace collection. Highlights include the Jadeite Cabbage and the Meat-Shaped Stone.

photo.alt: National Palace Museum complex on its hillside, Taipei
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-palace-museum-taipei.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "NationalPalace MuseumFrontView.jpg" by Peellden · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NationalPalace_MuseumFrontView.jpg -->

entries.name: Louvre Abu Dhabi
entries.desc: An encyclopedic museum created through an agreement with France, under a vast latticed dome by Jean Nouvel that filters light as a "rain of light" over galleries linking world cultures.

photo.alt: Sunlight filtering through the latticed dome over the reflecting pool at Louvre Abu Dhabi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: louvre-abu-dhabi.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Louvre abu dhabi, interno, cortile coperto 02" by Francesco Bini · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Louvre_abu_dhabi,_interno,_cortile_coperto_02.jpg -->

entries.name: British Museum
entries.desc: A vast survey of human history and culture, holding some eight million objects. Highlights include the Rosetta Stone, the Parthenon (Elgin) Marbles, and Egyptian mummies.

photo.alt: Neoclassical facade and columned entrance of the British Museum, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: british-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "British Museum London 2016 Facade 05.JPG" by Paasikivi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:British_Museum_London_2016_Facade_05.JPG -->

entries.name: National Gallery
entries.desc: Overlooking Trafalgar Square, it holds a comprehensive collection of Western European painting from the 13th to the early 20th century, with works by Van Eyck, Turner, and Van Gogh.

photo.alt: National Gallery facade on Trafalgar Square, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-gallery-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "National Gallery Facade.JPG" by Nathan Stazicker · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Gallery_Facade.JPG -->

entries.name: Tate Modern
entries.desc: Britain's national museum of modern and contemporary art, converted from the former Bankside Power Station on the Thames. The vast Turbine Hall hosts large-scale commissioned installations.

photo.alt: Tate Modern in the former Bankside Power Station, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tate-modern-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Tate Modern 0052.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tate_Modern_0052.JPG -->

entries.name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
entries.tag: New York City · Encyclopedic · All eras
entries.desc: The largest art museum in the Americas, lining Central Park's Museum Mile. Its encyclopedic collection ranges from Egyptian antiquities and European paintings to American and modern art.

photo.alt: The Metropolitan Museum of Art facade, New York City
photo.credit: <!-- photo: metropolitan-museum-of-art-nyc.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "The MET.jpg" by Kai Pilger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_MET.jpg -->

entries.name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA)
entries.desc: A defining institution of modern art in Midtown Manhattan, holding landmark works such as Van Gogh's The Starry Night and Picasso's Les Demoiselles d'Avignon.

photo.alt: Museum of Modern Art building, Midtown Manhattan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-of-modern-art-nyc.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "MoMa NY USA 1.jpg" by hibino · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:MoMa_NY_USA_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
entries.desc: Famous for its spiraling Frank Lloyd Wright rotunda, it holds a collection of modern and contemporary art, with strengths in Impressionism, Post-Impressionism, and abstract work.

photo.alt: Exterior of the spiral Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum building on Fifth Avenue, New York City
photo.credit: <!-- photo: guggenheim-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum (48059131351).jpg" by Ajay Suresh · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Solomon_R._Guggenheim_Museum_(48059131351).jpg -->

entries.name: Art Institute of Chicago
entries.desc: One of the largest US art museums, known for its French Impressionist holdings and iconic American works, including Grant Wood's American Gothic and Hopper's Nighthawks.

photo.alt: Exterior facade of the Art Institute of Chicago
photo.credit: <!-- photo: art-institute-of-chicago-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Art Institute of Chicago (51575570710)" by ajay_suresh · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Art_Institute_of_Chicago_(51575570710).jpg -->

meta: One of the Largest Art Museums in the US

entries.name: National Gallery of Art
entries.desc: On the National Mall, it surveys Western art from the Middle Ages to the present and holds the only Leonardo da Vinci painting in the Americas. Admission is free.

photo.alt: Neoclassical facade of the National Gallery of Art West Building in Washington, D.C.
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-gallery-of-art-west-building.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "National Gallery of Art - West Building facade" by AgnosticPreachersKid · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Gallery_of_Art_-_West_Building_facade.JPG -->

meta: Only da Vinci Painting in the Americas

entries.name: Getty Center
entries.tag: Los Angeles · European Old Masters · Photography
entries.desc: A hilltop campus of Richard Meier travertine pavilions and gardens, holding European paintings, drawings, sculpture, and photography. Admission is free.

photo.alt: The Getty Center's travertine campus in Los Angeles
photo.credit: <!-- photo: getty-center-los-angeles.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Getty Center, Los Angeles, California (32)" by Ken Lund · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Getty_Center,_Los_Angeles,_California_(32)_(3125794260).jpg -->

entries.name: Philadelphia Museum of Art
entries.desc: Set in a Greek Revival building whose entrance steps were made famous by the film Rocky. Its collection spans European, American, and Asian art across two millennia.

photo.alt: Greek Revival facade and steps of the Philadelphia Museum of Art
photo.credit: <!-- photo: philadelphia-museum-of-art.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Philadelphia Museum of Art Pennsylvania USA" by su1droot · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Philadelphia_Museum_of_Art_Pennsylvania_USA.jpg -->

entries.name: Museum of Fine Arts
entries.desc: One of the largest museums in the United States, with encyclopedic holdings notable for French Impressionism, ancient Egyptian works, and one of the finest Asian art collections in the West.

photo.alt: Huntington Avenue facade of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-of-fine-arts-boston-facade.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "2018 Museum of Fine Arts Boston Huntington Avenue facade from east" by Beyond My Ken · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2018_Museum_of_Fine_Arts_Boston_Huntington_Avenue_facade_from_east.jpg -->

meta: One of the Largest Museums in the US

entries.name: Whitney Museum of American Art
entries.tag: New York City · American Art · Contemporary
entries.desc: Focused on 20th- and 21st-century American art, now housed in a Renzo Piano building in the Meatpacking District. It is the home of the recurring Whitney Biennial.

photo.alt: The Whitney Museum of American Art's Renzo Piano building in the Meatpacking District
photo.credit: <!-- photo: whitney-museum-of-american-art.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "The Whitney Museum, New York City in 2015" by MusikAnimal · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Whitney_Museum,_New_York_City_in_2015.JPG -->

entries.name: Los Angeles County Museum of Art (LACMA)
entries.desc: The largest art museum in the western United States, with encyclopedic holdings spanning global cultures and eras. Chris Burden's Urban Light installation stands at its entrance.

photo.alt: Los Angeles County Museum of Art seen from Wilshire Boulevard
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lacma-wilshire-boulevard.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "LACMA" by The Angels 2010 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:LACMA.JPG -->

meta: Largest Art Museum in the Western US
